candidates.evidence_quote: Comparable sales increased 1.6% versus the prior year

traffic was a negative 0.8, but really that was, I would say, related to July. I think what everybody saw, we were improving throughout the quarter, flattish in August, and then positive traffic in September
cost of sales decreased 90 basis points, primarily driven by higher menu pricing than commodity inflation
a party of six or eight, the per capita spend is about 20% less than a party of two. And so that is just rebalancing
we expect effective commodity inflation of low single digits for Q4 as our broad market basket remains very stable
We are modeling net total labor inflation of low to mid single digits when factoring in the latest trends in wage rates and minimum wage increases
the latest trends in wage rates and minimum wage increases as well as other components of labor
Labor as a percent of sales decreased 40 basis points, primarily supported by menu pricing leverage relative to labor inflation and labor productivity improvements
partially offset by higher health insurance costs, mostly related to high cost claims in the quarter
Cheesecake factory restaurant level margins for the quarter improved by 180 basis points from Q3 of 2023
restaurant-level margin for the adjusted mature North Italia locations was 15%, a 250 basis point improvement from the prior year
lower food waste, which are contributing to improved restaurant level margins
Cheesecake Factory off-premise sales remain stable at 21% of sales for the third quarter
North Italia third quarter comparable sales increased 2% from the prior year
the Fire Child comps continue to be the strongest in our system, running mid-single digits
Flower Child average weekly sales were $85,200, up more than 6% from the third quarter of 2023
And external bakery sales were $14.9 million
we did just close a pretty good long-term agreement with a very prominent grocery chain that has about 2,000 locations
our preliminary capex estimate for the initial phase of development for the third bakery facility
we continue to be very pleased with the performance of the Cheesecake Rewards Program. Demand continues to surpass our internal expectations
record high guest satisfaction scores across all dine-in and to-go metrics
the stability of our staff retention and management retention and the restaurants has led to great execution
With 17 restaurant openings so far this year, we're well positioned to meet our objective of opening as many as 22 new restaurants in 2024
One of them was actually a site condemnation
Pre-opening costs were $7 million in the quarter compared to $6.7 million in the prior year period
we recorded pre-tax net income of $2.5 million primarily related to lease terminations
we continue to anticipate approximately $180 million to $200 million in cash capex to support this year's and some of next year's unit development
we completed approximately $1.1 million in share repurchases
returned $13.1 million to shareholders via our dividend
For Q4, we anticipate total revenues to be between $905 and $915 million
we would anticipate adjusted net income margin to be about 4.8% to 4.9% based on the sales range we provided
Total revenues of $865 million finished towards the higher end of the range we provided
Adjusted net income margin of 3.3% exceeded the high end of the guidance we provided
to have G&A as a percentage of sales decrease by a tenth
we are assuming a 10% to 11% tax rate
one of your shareholders is pushing for you to possibly separate some of the growth brands from the court cheesecake factory business
the deal we have with DoorDash covers all of the different concepts
comparable sales and traffic meaningfully exceeding the black box casual dining index by 310 and 380 basis points respectively
the two new markets that we entered into this year
challenges in terms of permitting and utilities
Depreciation is estimated to be approximately $26 million

notes: Chunk contains a single event: part 1 of 2 of the Q3 FY2024 earnings call transcript (CAKE_2024-10-29T17.00); names coined only from this visible part.
No fiscal_kpis in this chunk (KPIs appear only on chunk_001).
All 41 evidence quotes were programmatically verified as exact substrings of the event content.

Skipped (8): consumer income-cohort behavior (described as stable/consistent, vague), holiday private dining bookings (no change reported), shortened Black Friday-Christmas shopping season (management dismissed as a retail issue), 'better than expected flow through' (vague), debt/liquidity levels (reported unchanged), adjusted EPS 49% growth (YoY growth only, no consensus comparison to ground eps_surprise), supplier contract negotiations (folded into commodity_inflation), portfolio scale/synergy benefits (folded into general_and_administrative_expense and supply-chain items).
Brand-level metrics (north_italia_*, flower_child_*) kept separate from company-wide forms per ontology R9.
